title: Build Consultation Execution Features: Online (Secure Video, Chat, File Upload, e-Prescription) & Home Visit (Live Tracking, ETA, Contact Doctor)

requirements.text: Create a new page `frontend/src/pages/OnlineConsultationPage.tsx` at route `/online-consultation` that serves as the consultation execution screen for Online consultation type. The page must receive `doctorId`, `bookingId`, `date`, `timeSlot`, and `patientName` as URL search params. The screen must display a header with the doctor's name and specialty (looked up from mock data via doctorId), booking ID, and appointment time. Register this route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` following the existing TanStack Router pattern.

quotes: Online: Secure Video Chat File Upload e-Prescription

acceptanceCriteria: Route `/online-consultation` is registered in App.tsx and renders OnlineConsultationPage
Page reads doctorId, bookingId, date, timeSlot, and patientName from URL search params
Doctor name and specialty are displayed in the header using mock data lookup
Booking ID and appointment time are prominently displayed

requirements.text: On the OnlineConsultationPage, render a Secure Video section as the primary content area. Display a mock video call interface with a large video placeholder area (showing the doctor's avatar and name), a patient self-view thumbnail (bottom-right corner), and a control bar at the bottom with the following toggle buttons: 🎤 Mute/Unmute microphone, 📷 Camera on/off, 📞 End Call (red, navigates back to Booking Confirmation or Home). The video area must show a 'Waiting for doctor to join…' status message before the call is active and 'Connected' once the mock call starts (auto-transition after 3 seconds). Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.
acceptanceCriteria: Large video placeholder area displays doctor avatar and name
Patient self-view thumbnail is shown in the bottom-right corner
Control bar has Mute, Camera, and End Call buttons
End Call button is red and navigates away from the consultation screen
Status message shows 'Waiting for doctor to join…' initially, transitions to 'Connected' after 3 seconds
Styling follows the Medical Blue theme

requirements.text: On the OnlineConsultationPage, add a collapsible Chat panel (slide-in from the right or as a tab below the video area). The chat panel must display a message thread area (initially empty with a placeholder 'No messages yet'), a text input field at the bottom, and a Send button. Clicking Send appends the typed message to the thread as a patient message (right-aligned, Medical Blue background) in local component state. Mock doctor replies (left-aligned, grey background) must appear automatically 2 seconds after each patient message with a static response such as 'Thank you, I have noted that.' The chat toggle button must be accessible from the video control bar (💬 icon). Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat panel can be toggled open/closed from the video control bar
Message thread shows patient messages right-aligned in Medical Blue and doctor replies left-aligned in grey
Mock doctor reply appears 2 seconds after each patient message
Send button appends the typed message to the thread
Input field is cleared after sending
Placeholder text shown when no messages exist

requirements.text: On the OnlineConsultationPage, add a File Upload section accessible via a 📎 icon button in the video control bar or chat panel. Clicking the icon opens a file picker (accept PDF, JPG, PNG). Selected files are listed below the chat input with their filename and size. Each uploaded file displays a remove (×) button. Files are stored in local component state only (no backend upload). A maximum of 5 files may be attached at once; exceeding the limit shows an inline error message. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.
acceptanceCriteria: File picker accepts PDF, JPG, PNG formats
Selected file names and sizes are listed in the UI
Each file has a remove button that deselects it
Maximum of 5 files enforced with an inline error message when exceeded
Files are managed in local component state only

requirements.text: On the OnlineConsultationPage, add an e-Prescription section accessible via a 📋 tab or button (labelled 'e-Prescription'). The section must display a prescription form with the following read-only pre-filled fields: Patient Name, Doctor Name, Date; and the following editable fields: Diagnosis (text area), Medicines (a dynamic list where each entry has: medicine name text input, dosage text input, frequency select — Once/Twice/Thrice daily, duration text input, and a remove row button), and Additional Notes (text area). An 'Add Medicine' button appends a new empty medicine row. A 'Generate Prescription' button at the bottom renders the completed prescription as a styled summary card (mock PDF preview) and shows a 'Download PDF' button (which triggers a browser print dialog). Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.
acceptanceCriteria: Patient name, doctor name, and date are pre-filled and read-only
Diagnosis text area, dynamic medicines list, and additional notes are editable
Add Medicine button appends a new row with medicine name, dosage, frequency, duration, and remove button
Generate Prescription button renders a styled prescription summary card
Download PDF button triggers the browser print dialog
Styling follows the Medical Blue theme

requirements.text: Create a new page `frontend/src/pages/HomeVisitTrackingPage.tsx` at route `/home-visit-tracking` that serves as the consultation execution screen for Home Visit consultation type. The page must receive `doctorId`, `bookingId`, `date`, `timeSlot`, and `patientName` as URL search params. The screen must display a header with the doctor's name, specialty, and booking ID. Register this route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` following the existing TanStack Router pattern. From the Booking Confirmation page (`frontend/src/pages/BookingConfirmationPage.tsx`), when `consultationType` is 'Home Visit', the 'Join / Track Visit' button (or equivalent CTA) must navigate to `/home-visit-tracking` with the appropriate query params.

quotes: Home Visit: Live Tracking ETA Contact Doctor

acceptanceCriteria: Route `/home-visit-tracking` is registered in App.tsx and renders HomeVisitTrackingPage
Page reads doctorId, bookingId, date, timeSlot, and patientName from URL search params
Doctor name, specialty, and booking ID are displayed in the header
Booking Confirmation page navigates to this route when consultationType is 'Home Visit'

requirements.text: On the HomeVisitTrackingPage, render a Live Tracking section as the primary content area. Display a mock map-style tracking UI consisting of: a rectangular placeholder area (styled with a subtle grid/map background) labelled 'Live Map', a doctor avatar marker icon on the map that animates (CSS transition) from one position to another every 5 seconds to simulate movement, a patient home marker icon at a fixed position, and a dashed route line between the two markers. Below the map, display a status banner that cycles through 'Doctor has accepted your request', 'Doctor is on the way', and 'Doctor is nearby' at 8-second intervals to simulate real-time status updates. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.
acceptanceCriteria: Map placeholder area is rendered with a grid/map background
Doctor marker animates position every 5 seconds using CSS transitions
Patient home marker is fixed in place
Dashed route line connects the two markers
Status banner cycles through three status messages at 8-second intervals
Styling follows the Medical Blue theme

requirements.text: On the HomeVisitTrackingPage, display an ETA (Estimated Time of Arrival) section prominently below the map. Show a large countdown timer that starts at a mock value of 18 minutes and counts down to 0, formatted as 'MM:SS'. Display a label 'Estimated Arrival' above the timer and a sub-label 'Doctor is on the way' below it. When the countdown reaches 0, replace the timer with the message 'Doctor has arrived!' styled as a success banner in green/Medical Blue. The countdown must be implemented using `setInterval` / `useEffect`. Styling must follow the Medical Blue theme.
acceptanceCriteria: ETA countdown starts at 18:00 and counts down to 0 using setInterval/useEffect
Timer is formatted as MM:SS
Label 'Estimated Arrival' appears above and 'Doctor is on the way' below the timer
When countdown reaches 0, timer is replaced with a 'Doctor has arrived!' success banner
Styling follows the Medical Blue theme

requirements.text: On the HomeVisitTrackingPage, add a 'Contact Doctor' section below the ETA section. It must display two action buttons: (1) 📞 Call Doctor — displays a modal/popover with the mock doctor phone number (e.g., '+91 98765 XXXXX') and a 'Close' button; (2) 💬 Message Doctor — opens an inline chat panel identical in behaviour to the chat panel described in REQ-47 (local state only, mock auto-reply after 2 seconds). Both buttons must be styled prominently in the Medical Blue theme. Additionally, display the doctor's name, photo/avatar, and a 'Verified Doctor' badge in this section.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Call Doctor' button opens a modal/popover showing a mock phone number and a Close button
'Message Doctor' button opens an inline chat panel with local state and mock auto-reply
Doctor name, avatar/photo placeholder, and 'Verified Doctor' badge are displayed
Both buttons are styled in the Medical Blue theme

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/pages/BookingConfirmationPage.tsx` to add consultation execution entry-point buttons below the AppointmentReminders section and above the navigation buttons. For `consultationType === 'Online'`, display a 'Start Consultation' button (visible and enabled only when current time is within 15 minutes of the appointment, using the same `setInterval`/`useEffect` pattern already used for the 'Join Video Call' button) that navigates to `/online-consultation` with doctorId, bookingId, date, timeSlot, and patientName as query params. For `consultationType === 'Home Visit'`, display a 'Track Doctor' button (always visible once booking is confirmed) that navigates to `/home-visit-tracking` with the same query params. Both buttons must be styled in the Medical Blue theme.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Start Consultation' button appears for Online type and is enabled only within 15 minutes of appointment time
'Track Doctor' button appears for Home Visit type and is always visible after booking
Both buttons navigate to their respective pages with the correct query params
Both buttons are styled in the Medical Blue theme
Buttons appear below the AppointmentReminders section and above the 'Go to Home'/'View My Bookings' buttons

constraints: No real video SDK, payment gateway, or map API integration — all consultation and tracking features must use mock/simulated data and client-side state only
All new pages must be registered in frontend/src/App.tsx following the existing TanStack Router pattern
Do not modify any files listed under frontend.immutablePaths
Reuse the existing mock doctor dataset from frontend/src/lib/mockDoctors.ts as the single source of truth for doctor data
Styling must consistently follow the existing Medical Blue theme defined in frontend/index.css and frontend/tailwind.config.js
nonGoals: Real WebRTC or video SDK integration (e.g., Twilio, Agora, Daily.co)
Real GPS or map API integration (e.g., Google Maps, Mapbox)
Backend persistence of consultation sessions, chat messages, or prescriptions
Real file upload to backend or cloud storage
SMS, email, or push notification delivery
Razorpay or any real payment gateway integration
Post-consultation features (ratings, follow-up, lab test orders from e-Prescription)
